title: Small Mortar and Pestle Set - Non Slip Silicone and Ceramic Pestle and Mortar Bowl - White and Black Grinder and Herb Crusher with No Slip Silicone Base
description:
About this item
Non slip gripped handle and base - Our mortar and pestle set has a silicone bowl to keep it still while you use it. The pestle has a silicone handle to make it easier to grind.
Apothecary grade porcelain - Pharmacies traditionally use non-porous porcelain to grind medication - with great reason. Wood absorbs the taste of previous grinds. Marble or granite or stone create powder! Stone is hard to clean (can't use soap!)
Great for: powdering herbs, grains, spices, nuts, seeds, teas, bread, biscuits (for making homemade bread crumbs!), or crushing medicines for pets and humans alike. Ideal for unlocking the hidden oils in your everyday dry spices as a spice grinder.
Useful in any home kitchen, or as a pill grinder to crush medication, or as a grinder bowl for spices and seeds, and guacamole bowl - 4.5 inches across, 3.25 inches tall; pestle - 5.5 inches tall. Add to cart now!
reviews_5s:
I chose the ceramic with unglazed grinding surfaces to effectively smash and mix things without trailing color or smell; this item did not disappoint.

However, the features is this particular mortar and pestle of that sort really saved the day. I have always wanted one of these, but had never taken the plunge. This one caught my eye because it's weighted and non-slip, just seemed like a good idea. White making s chili paste with garlic cloves and various spices, my enthusiasm outpaced my skill with this new tool. Only the weighted button and the non-slip silicone footprint saved me from losing control if both pieces and their contents. I doubt I would have had both mortar and pestle survive the flying smash my inexperience would have created.

The silicone assists with grip on both pieces, and the effectiveness for grinding and smashing and mixing ingredients is as expected for this type of tool. Also, the item is well constructed and blissfully easy to clean without any residual color or smell--it looks brand new after the first use.

I feel like it's a good visit for the money given the overall design and function--especially if you're not familiar with using one of these.

I'm someone who likes to get something completely clean so this fits the bill. I'm also not keen on seasoning a tool that's not exposed to heat so the unglazed ceramic properties are perfect for my needs.

[That said, I still can't determine where it was made between the box, insert, and markings absent from the item.]I am satisfied with my Cooler Kitchen Mortar and Pestle. The Mortar and Pestle was as advertised, and was consistent with the reviews I read prior to purchase. It is the right size for my needs. The rough sides on the inside aid greatly in grinding things and kind of holds things so you can grind. It has some weight which is good for its intended purpose since you will be exerting some force while grinding, and the weight keeps it in place, but it's weight does not affect it's ease of use. The non slip coverings made the pestle easy to hold, and stopped the Mortar from moving during use. It is easy to use and easy to clean. It has solid, simple, no nonsense, clean lines to do what it was meant to do, but I think it also looks great! Just a well thought out, well designed, and well made product (obviously from people who cook). I would recommend it to others. A welcome addition to my kitchen that will be used!Beautifully designed and crafted - a piece of art that you will want to keep on the countertop and not hidden away in a cabinet. Heavy weight and the silicon base adds stability. This is an excellent gift ... for others and yourself!Got this after using my sister in laws. Like that it solid and easy to use. Good quality.I have hesitated to spend the money for a mortar & pestle for a long time, but finally took the plunge. I am so happy with this set… it was larger & heavier than I was envisioning, but is so solid and efficient, easy to rinse clean and does the job in no time! It has made taking difficult-to-swallow tablets a breeze as I turn them into powder and eat them with something puréed like applesauce or yogurt. I haven’t tried a pesto yet, but that’s my next task! LOVE this quality item from Cooler Kitchen!I often need to grind medicine for my super picky cat, and this mortar and Pestle Set works perfectly. It grinds to a fine powder and is easy to clean.Purchased this item to use for herbs but when it came I was pleasantly surprised. It will handle more. It is durable, stays in place due to the non skid silicone bottom and the hand piece is easy grip so your hand doesnt slip. I have been using this for a lot of culinary cooking. It's a must have!Really happy with the build and quality of the productEasy to use and clean . love mineCrushes tablets into powder and does it well! Hint: Do NOT hammer the mortar (bowl) with the pestle (not a hammer). Press and grind.
reviews_1s:
Used it get a few cardamom pods ground and to my surprise, heard a sharp pitched sound after I applied the second pound to grind. Saw a hole on the inner left bottom portion of the mortar when I cleared the cardamom pods aside. I hardly even used any strength and this thing broke!!! Lifted the mortar and a quarter of the base that had cracked with whatever little impact I applied fell apart like a broken egg shell!!!

Bought this reading all the great reviews, but should have gone old school and stuck to the traditional stone or wood mortars. To be honest, even before I bought this, my gut didn't feel right because it was a porcelain mortar and I was skeptical about how well porcelain would do the job. They might as well have made this from glass!!

Also, when I got this and I carefully inspected it the first time around, I noticed that there were some patches on the product that look like it had been painted over - like when you fill cement in holes and paint over. It may work for light duty guacomole, leaves or minced coconut - but not sure how it will fare for grinding spices.

If your cooking needs you to grind whole spices (cardamom, peppercorn, coriander seeds) please don't buy this. I am quite surprised by what the reviews said, and how this product performed.
Crack a few cardamom pods to test!!I did the research and found that other versions made of granite or marble supposedly absorb flavors and have a problem with stuff sticking to them, so porcelain made a lot of sense and i went with this product........the inside bowl is only smooth porcelain for the first half inch or so....then it is unfinished porcelain that is actually pretty rough and absolutely everything sticks to that surface very badly. You can't crush up a pill without half the pill sticking to the crushing part and the other half sticking to the bowl itself......very disappointed and cannot figure out why they left that part unfinished, it honestly makes the product unusable.This item is only a few months old and all I have used it for is to grind a couple of pills each day (for my dog). Somehow it is already cracked and has a small chip out of the side. It has not moved from my kitchen counter since I started using it (it arrived in good shape), so I don't know why it is cracked from only grinding a couple of pills.

I certainly would not purchase it again.It arrived with a small hole in the pestle. I wrote the company about it but they never got back with me with a solution after initially saying that they would. A note that came with the item requested direct correspondence with the company if there were any problems since they are smaller and family-run. I can't use it with a pit in the head of the pestle that would accumulate ground up matter and be hard to clean.the inside is very porous and it takes tedious cleaning to get all of your crushed meds out, & you still dont get it all out. if you have pills you cant or shouldnt crush together then you must wash thoroughly after each different med, talk about a painn in the rear. i 100% do not recommend this for those reason.Today I finally got around to using this mortar and pestle and boy was I disappointed.
I was making a paste for a marinade which called for black peppercorns, cilantro stems, and garlic. Long before the ingredients could be turned into a paste the bottom of the mortar burst right out. This product may be good for grinding up pills but it isn't useful in the kitchen.Unreasonably bad build quality for a mortar and pestle. Bought in June and used it a few times. Was grinding allspice cloves today and the bottom gave out entirely, spraying porcelain fragments all over the countertop. Unlike a solid granite one with a granite base, this mortar and pestle is hollow underneath the rubber.I bought it about 3 months ago. Rarely used it. Two days ago when my wife used it to crush some ginger, it broke at the bottom. A piece just broke off leaving a hole at the bottom. Pictures are attached.Lightly used for 5 years. First time I go to mash garlic, pop goes the bottom.

It needs to be solid, ridge reinforced, or potted to support the load.I bought it to crush garlic. At my second use only, the bottom of it broke off, leaving it with a huge hole at its bottom.
